tools.name: cpp_semantic_query
tools.description: Run a normalized semantic query over C++ code entities, scopes, filters, and relations. IMPORTANT PARAMETER GUIDANCE: 'scope' controls WHERE to look (file, directory, or glob pattern goes in scope.path). 'where' controls WHAT to match (name, return_type, access, etc.). Never put file paths in 'where'. Use the 'fields' parameter to limit which fields are returned per item and keep responses small. Set "include_source": true to retrieve the full source text and line extent for each matched symbol. Example: action=list, entity=method, scope={path:"src/dir"}, where={return_type:"uint32_t"}, fields=["symbol_id","qualified_name"]. Important: entity=method returns regular methods only and excludes constructors and destructors. To list all member functions of a class, run three queries and merge results: entity=method, entity=constructor, and entity=destructor. scope.path must be a workspace-root-relative path (e.g. "src/RecBoardInterface.h", directory "src/", or glob "src/*.h") or an absolute path. Bare filenames like "RecBoardInterface.h" will fail — use file_search first. SCALABILITY WARNING: The backend parses each file from scratch with no caching. For inverse-relation queries like 'called_by', ALWAYS grep/ripgrep for the function name first to find candidate files, then run the semantic query scoped to only those files. Even moderate codebases (50+ files in scope) become noticeably slow without grep pre-filtering. Rule of thumb: if a 'called_by' scope could match more than ~20 files, grep first to narrow it down.
